api/QuartzRestApi.Controllers.SchedulerC.summary: Class SchedulerController Namespace QuartzRestApi.Controllers Assembly QuartzRestApi.dll Provides endpoints for managing and monitoring a Quartz.NET IScheduler instance. This controller exposes various operations such as checking the status of job and trigger groups, retrieving scheduler metadata, and managing jobs and triggers. SchedulerController(IScheduler scheduler, ILogger logger = null) Parameters scheduler IScheduler Quartz.IScheduler logger ILogger ILogger Methods AddCalendar(string) Add (register) the given Quartz.ICalendar to the Scheduler. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/AddCalendar")] public Task AddCalendar(string json) Parameters json string The Quartz.ICalendar information Returns Task AddJob(string) Add the given Quartz.IJob to the Scheduler - with no associated Quartz.ITrigger. The Quartz.IJob will be 'dormant' until it is scheduled with a Quartz.ITrigger, or TriggerJob /> is called for it. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/AddJob")] public Task AddJob(string json) Parameters json string Returns Task Remarks With the StoreNonDurableWhileAwaitingScheduling parameter set to true , a non-durable job can be stored. Once it is scheduled, it will resume normal non-durable behavior (i.e. be deleted once there are no remaining associated triggers). CheckExistsJobKey(string) Determine whether a Quartz.IJob with the given identifier already exists within the scheduler. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/checkexistsjobkey")] public bool CheckExistsJobKey(string json) Parameters json string the identifier to check for Returns bool true if a Job exists with the given identifier CheckExistsTriggerKey(string) Determine whether a Quartz.ITrigger with the given identifier already exists within the scheduler. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/checkexiststriggerkey")] public bool CheckExistsTriggerKey(string json) Parameters json string the identifier to check for Returns bool true if a Trigger exists with the given identifier Clear() Clears (deletes!) all scheduling data - all Quartz.IJobs, Quartz.ITriggers Quartz.ICalendars. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/Clear")] public void Clear() Context() Returns the Quartz.SchedulerContext of the Quartz.IScheduler. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/SchedulerContext")] public string Context() Returns string DeleteCalendar(string) Delete the identified Quartz.ICalendar from the Scheduler. [HttpDelete] [Route("Scheduler/DeleteCalendar/{calName}")] public bool DeleteCalendar(string calName) Parameters calName string Name of the calendar. Returns bool true if the Calendar was found and deleted. Remarks If removal of the Calendar would result in Quartz.ITriggers pointing to non-existent calendars, then a Quartz.SchedulerException will be thrown. DeleteJob(string) Delete the identified Quartz.IJob from the Scheduler - and any associated Quartz.ITriggers. [HttpDelete] [Route("Scheduler/DeleteJob")] public Task<bool> DeleteJob(string json) Parameters json string Returns Task<bool> true if the Job was found and deleted. DeleteJobs(string) Delete the identified jobs from the Scheduler - and any associated Quartz.ITriggers. [HttpDelete] [Route("Scheduler/DeleteJobs")] public Task<bool> DeleteJobs(string json) Parameters json string Returns Task<bool> true if all the Jobs were found and deleted, false if one or more were not deleted. Remarks Note that while this bulk operation is likely more efficient than invoking DeleteJob(string) several times, it may have the adverse affect of holding data locks for a single long duration of time (rather than lots of small durations of time). GetCalendar(string) Get the Quartz.ICalendar instance with the given name. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/Getcalendar/{calName}")] public string GetCalendar(string calName) Parameters calName string Returns string GetCalendarNames() Get the names of all registered Quartz.ICalendar. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/GetCalendarNames")] public Task<IReadOnlyCollection<string>> GetCalendarNames() Returns Task<IReadOnlyCollection<string>> GetCurrentlyExecutingJobs() Return a list of Quartz.IJobExecutionContext objects that represent all currently executing Jobs in this Scheduler instance. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/GetCurrentlyExecutingJobs")] public string GetCurrentlyExecutingJobs() Returns string Remarks This method is not cluster aware. That is, it will only return Jobs currently executing in this Scheduler instance, not across the entire cluster. Note that the list returned is an 'instantaneous' snapshot, and that as soon as it's returned, the true list of executing jobs may be different. Also, please read the doc associated with Quartz.IJobExecutionContext- especially if you're using remoting. See Also IJobExecutionContext GetJobDetail(string) Get the Quartz.IJobDetail for the Quartz.IJob instance with the given key. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/GetJobDetail")] public string GetJobDetail(string json) Parameters json string Returns string Remarks The returned JobDetail object will be a snapshot of the actual stored JobDetail. If you wish to modify the JobDetail, you must re-store the JobDetail afterward (e.g. see AddJob(string)). GetJobGroupNames() Get the names of all known Quartz.IJobDetail groups. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/GetJobGroupNames")] public Task<IReadOnlyCollection<string>> GetJobGroupNames() Returns Task<IReadOnlyCollection<string>> GetJobKeys(string) Get the keys of all the Quartz.IJobDetails in the matching groups. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/GetJobKeys")] public string GetJobKeys(string json) Parameters json string Returns string GetMetaData() Get a Quartz.SchedulerMetaData object describing the settings and capabilities of the scheduler instance. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/GetMetaData")] public string GetMetaData() Returns string Remarks Note that the data returned is an 'instantaneous' snapshot, and that as soon as it's returned, the meta-data values may be different. GetPausedTriggerGroups() Get the names of all Quartz.ITrigger groups that are paused. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/GetPausedTriggerGroups")] public Task<IReadOnlyCollection<string>> GetPausedTriggerGroups() Returns Task<IReadOnlyCollection<string>> GetTrigger(string) Get the Quartz.ITrigger instance with the given key. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/GetTrigger")] public string GetTrigger(string json) Parameters json string Returns string Remarks The returned Trigger object will be a snapshot of the actual stored trigger. If you wish to modify the trigger, you must re-store the trigger afterward (e.g. see RescheduleJob(string)). GetTriggerGroupNames() Get the names of all known Quartz.ITrigger groups. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/GetTriggerGroupNames")] public Task<IReadOnlyCollection<string>> GetTriggerGroupNames() Returns Task<IReadOnlyCollection<string>> GetTriggerKeys(string) Get the names of all the Quartz.ITriggers in the given groups. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/GetTriggerKeys")] public string GetTriggerKeys(string json) Parameters json string Returns string GetTriggerState(string) Get the current state of the identified Quartz.ITrigger. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/GetTriggerState")] public string GetTriggerState(string json) Parameters json string Returns string See Also Normal Paused Complete Blocked Error None GetTriggersOfJob(string) Get all Quartz.ITrigger s that are associated with the identified Quartz.IJobDetail. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/GetTriggersOfJob")] public string GetTriggersOfJob(string json) Parameters json string Returns string Remarks The returned Trigger objects will be snapshots of the actual stored triggers. If you wish to modify a trigger, you must re-store the trigger afterward (e.g. see RescheduleJob(string)). InStandbyMode() Reports whether the Quartz.IScheduler is in stand-by mode. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/InStandbyMode")] public bool InStandbyMode() Returns bool See Also Standby() Start() InterruptFireInstanceId(string) Request the cancellation, within this Scheduler instance, of the identified executing job instance. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/interruptfireinstanceid/{fireInstanceId}")] public bool InterruptFireInstanceId(string fireInstanceId) Parameters fireInstanceId string the unique identifier of the job instance to be interrupted (see Quartz.IJobExecutionContext.FireInstanceId) Returns bool true if the identified job instance was found and interrupted. Remarks This method is not cluster aware. That is, it will only interrupt instances of the identified InterruptableJob currently executing in this Scheduler instance, not across the entire cluster. See Also GetCurrentlyExecutingJobs() FireInstanceId InterruptJobKey(string) InterruptJobKey(string) Request the cancellation, within this Scheduler instance, of all currently executing instances of the identified Quartz.IJob. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/InterruptJobKey")] public bool InterruptJobKey(string json) Parameters json string Returns bool true is at least one instance of the identified job was found and interrupted. Remarks If more than one instance of the identified job is currently executing, the cancellation token will be set on each instance. However, there is a limitation that in the case that InterruptJobKey(string) on one instances throws an exception, all remaining instances (that have not yet been interrupted) will not have their InterruptJobKey(string) method called. If you wish to interrupt a specific instance of a job (when more than one is executing) you can do so by calling GetCurrentlyExecutingJobs() to obtain a handle to the job instance, and then invoke InterruptJobKey(string) on it yourself. This method is not cluster aware. That is, it will only interrupt instances of the identified InterruptableJob currently executing in this Scheduler instance, not across the entire cluster. See Also GetCurrentlyExecutingJobs() IsJobGroupPaused(string) Returns true if the given JobGroup is paused [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/IsJobGroupPaused/{groupName}")] public Task<bool> IsJobGroupPaused(string groupName) Parameters groupName string The group name Returns Task<bool> IsShutdown() Reports whether the Quartz.IScheduler has been Shutdown. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/Isshutdown")] public bool IsShutdown() Returns bool IsStarted() Whether the scheduler has been started. [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/IsStarted")] public bool IsStarted() Returns bool Remarks Note: This only reflects whether Start() has ever been called on this Scheduler, so it will return true even if the Quartz.IScheduler is currently in standby mode or has been since shutdown. See Also Start() IsShutdown() InStandbyMode() IsTriggerGroupPaused(string) Returns true if the given TriggerGroup is paused [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/IsTriggerGroupPaused/{groupName}")] public Task<bool> IsTriggerGroupPaused(string groupName) Parameters groupName string The group name Returns Task<bool> PauseAll() Pause all triggers - similar to calling PauseTriggers(string) on every group, however, after using this method ResumeAll() must be called to clear the scheduler's state of 'remembering' that all new triggers will be paused as they are added. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/PauseAllTriggers")] public void PauseAll() Remarks When ResumeAll() is called (to un-pause), trigger misfire instructions WILL be applied. See Also ResumeAll() PauseTriggers(string) Standby() PauseJob(string) Pause the Quartz.IJobDetail with the given key - by pausing all of its current Quartz.ITriggers. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/PauseJob")] public Task PauseJob(string json) Parameters json string Returns Task PauseJobs(string) Pause all the Quartz.IJobDetails in the matching groups - by pausing all of their Quartz.ITriggers. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/PauseJobs")] public Task PauseJobs(string json) Parameters json string Returns Task Remarks The Scheduler will "remember" that the groups are paused, and impose the pause on any new jobs that are added to any of those groups until it is resumed. NOTE: There is a limitation that only exactly matched groups can be remembered as paused. For example, if there are pre-existing job in groups "aaa" and "bbb" and a matcher is given to pause groups that start with "a" then the group "aaa" will be remembered as paused and any subsequently added jobs in group "aaa" will be paused, however if a job is added to group "axx" it will not be paused, as "axx" wasn't known at the time the "group starts with a" matcher was applied. HOWEVER, if there are pre-existing groups "aaa" and "bbb" and a matcher is given to pause the group "axx" (with a group equals matcher) then no jobs will be paused, but it will be remembered that group "axx" is paused and later when a job is added in that group, it will become paused. See Also ResumeJobs(string) PauseTrigger(string) Pause the Quartz.ITrigger with the given key. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/PauseTrigger")] public Task PauseTrigger(string json) Parameters json string Returns Task PauseTriggers(string) Pause all the Quartz.ITriggers in the groups matching. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/PauseTriggers")] public Task PauseTriggers(string json) Parameters json string Returns Task Remarks The Scheduler will "remember" all the groups paused, and impose the pause on any new triggers that are added to any of those groups until it is resumed. NOTE: There is a limitation that only exactly matched groups can be remembered as paused. For example, if there are pre-existing triggers in groups "aaa" and "bbb" and a matcher is given to pause groups that start with "a" then the group "aaa" will be remembered as paused and any subsequently added triggers in that group be paused, however if a trigger is added to group "axx" it will not be paused, as "axx" wasn't known at the time the "group starts with a" matcher was applied. HOWEVER, if there are pre-existing groups "aaa" and "bbb" and a matcher is given to pause the group "axx" (with a group equals matcher) then no triggers will be paused, but it will be remembered that group "axx" is paused and later when a trigger is added in that group, it will become paused. See Also ResumeTriggers(string) RescheduleJob(string) Remove (delete) the Quartz.ITrigger with the given key, and store the new given one - which must be associated with the same job (the new trigger must have the job name & group specified) - however, the new trigger need not have the same name as the old trigger. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/RescheduleJob")] public Task<DateTimeOffset?> RescheduleJob(string json) Parameters json string The json Returns Task<DateTimeOffset?> null if a Quartz.ITrigger with the given name and group was not found and removed from the store (and the new trigger is therefore not stored), otherwise the first fire time of the newly scheduled trigger. ResetTriggerFromErrorState(string) Remove the given Quartz.ITrigger from the error state and set it back to Quartz.TriggerState.Normal or Quartz.TriggerState.Paused as appropriate. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/ResetTriggerFromErrorState")] public Task ResetTriggerFromErrorState(string json) Parameters json string Returns Task Remarks Only affects triggers that are in Quartz.TriggerState.Error state. If the trigger is not in that state, the call is a no-op. ResumeAll() Resume (un-pause) all triggers - similar to calling ResumeTriggers(string) on every group. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/ResumeAllTriggers")] public void ResumeAll() Remarks If any Quartz.ITrigger missed one or more fire-times, then the Quartz.ITrigger's misfire instruction will be applied. See Also PauseAll() ResumeJob(string) Resume (un-pause) the Quartz.IJobDetail with the given key. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/ResumeJob")] public Task ResumeJob(string json) Parameters json string Returns Task Remarks If any of the Quartz.IJob'sQuartz.ITrigger s missed one or more fire-times, then the Quartz.ITrigger's misfire instruction will be applied. ResumeJobs(string) Resume (un-pause) all the Quartz.IJobDetails in matching groups. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/ResumeJobs")] public Task ResumeJobs(string json) Parameters json string Returns Task Remarks If any of the Quartz.IJob s had Quartz.ITrigger s that missed one or more fire-times, then the Quartz.ITrigger's misfire instruction will be applied. See Also PauseJobs(string) ResumeTrigger(string) Resume (un-pause) the Quartz.ITrigger with the given key. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/ResumeTrigger")] public Task ResumeTrigger(string json) Parameters json string Returns Task Remarks If the Quartz.ITrigger missed one or more fire-times, then the Quartz.ITrigger's misfire instruction will be applied. ResumeTriggers(string) Resume (un-pause) all the Quartz.ITriggers in matching groups. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/ResumeTriggers")] public Task ResumeTriggers(string json) Parameters json string Returns Task Remarks If any Quartz.ITrigger missed one or more fire-times, then the Quartz.ITrigger's misfire instruction will be applied. See Also PauseTriggers(string) ScheduleJob(string) Add the given Quartz.IJobDetail to the Scheduler, and associate the given Quartz.ITrigger with it. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/ScheduleJobWithJobDetailAndTrigger")] public Task<DateTimeOffset> ScheduleJob(string json) Parameters json string Returns Task<DateTimeOffset> Remarks If the given Trigger does not reference any Quartz.IJob, then it will be set to reference the Job passed with it into this method. ScheduleJobIdentifiedWithTrigger(string) Schedule the given Quartz.ITrigger with the Quartz.IJob identified by the Quartz.ITrigger's settings. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/ScheduleJobIdentifiedWithTrigger")] public Task<DateTimeOffset> ScheduleJobIdentifiedWithTrigger(string json) Parameters json string Returns Task<DateTimeOffset> ScheduleJobWithTriggers(string) Schedule the given job with the related set of triggers. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/ScheduleJobWithJobDetailAndTriggers")] public Task ScheduleJobWithTriggers(string json) Parameters json string Returns Task Remarks If any of the given job or triggers already exist (or more specifically, if the keys are not unique) and the replace parameter is not set to true then an exception will be thrown. ScheduleJobs(string) Schedule all the given jobs with the related set of triggers. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/ScheduleJobs")] public Task ScheduleJobs(string json) Parameters json string Returns Task Remarks If any of the given jobs or triggers already exist (or more specifically, if the keys are not unique) and the replace parameter is not set to true then an exception will be thrown. SchedulerInstanceId() Returns the instance id of the scheduler [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/SchedulerInstanceId")] public string SchedulerInstanceId() Returns string SchedulerName() Returns the name of the scheduler [HttpGet] [Route("Scheduler/SchedulerName")] public string SchedulerName() Returns string Shutdown() Halts the Quartz.IScheduler's firing of Quartz.ITriggers, and cleans up all resources associated with the Scheduler. Equivalent to Shutdown(bool). [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/Shutdown")] public Task Shutdown() Returns Task Remarks The scheduler cannot be re-started. See Also Shutdown(bool) Shutdown(bool) Halts the Quartz.IScheduler's firing of Quartz.ITriggers, and cleans up all resources associated with the Scheduler. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/Shutdown/{waitForJobsToComplete}")] public Task Shutdown(bool waitForJobsToComplete) Parameters waitForJobsToComplete bool if true the scheduler will not allow this method to return until all currently executing jobs have completed. Returns Task Remarks The scheduler cannot be re-started. See Also Shutdown() Standby() Temporarily halts the Quartz.IScheduler's firing of Quartz.ITriggers. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/Standby")] public Task Standby() Returns Task Remarks When Start() is called (to bring the scheduler out of stand-by mode), trigger misfire instructions will NOT be applied during the execution of the Start() method - any misfires will be detected immediately afterward (by the Quartz.Spi.IJobStore's normal process). The scheduler is not destroyed, and can be re-started at any time. See Also Start() PauseAll() Start() Starts the Quartz.IScheduler's threads that fire Quartz.ITriggers. When a scheduler is first created it is in "stand-by" mode, and will not fire triggers. The scheduler can also be put into stand-by mode by calling the Standby() method. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/Start")] public Task Start() Returns Task Remarks The misfire/recovery process will be started, if it is the initial call to this method on this scheduler instance. See Also StartDelayed(int) Standby() Shutdown(bool) StartDelayed(int) Calls Start() after the indicated delay. (This call does not block). This can be useful within applications that have initializers that create the scheduler immediately, before the resources needed by the executing jobs have been fully initialized. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/StartDelayed/{delay}")] public Task StartDelayed(int delay) Parameters delay int Returns Task See Also Start() Standby() Shutdown(bool) TriggerJobWithDataMap(string) Trigger the identified Quartz.IJobDetail (Execute it now). [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/TriggerJobWithDataMap")] public Task TriggerJobWithDataMap(string json) Parameters json string The JobKey with associated Quartz.JobDataMap of the Quartz.IJob to be executed. Returns Task TriggerJobWithJobKey(string) Trigger the identified Quartz.IJobDetail (Execute it now). [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/TriggerJobWithJobkey")] public Task TriggerJobWithJobKey(string json) Parameters json string Returns Task UnscheduleJob(string) Remove the indicated Quartz.ITrigger from the scheduler. If the related job does not have any other triggers, and the job is not durable, then the job will also be deleted. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/UnscheduleJob")] public Task<bool> UnscheduleJob(string json) Parameters json string Returns Task<bool> UnscheduleJobs(string) Remove all the indicated Quartz.ITriggers from the scheduler. [HttpPost] [Route("Scheduler/UnscheduleJobs")] public Task<bool> UnscheduleJobs(string json) Parameters json string Returns Task<bool> Remarks If the related job does not have any other triggers, and the job is not durable, then the job will also be deleted. Note that while this bulk operation is likely more efficient than invoking UnscheduleJob(string) several times, it may have the adverse affect of holding data locks for a single long duration of time (rather than lots of small durations of time).

api/QuartzRestApi.SchedulerHost.html.title: Class SchedulerHost | QuartzRestApi
api/QuartzRestApi.SchedulerHost.html.summary: Class SchedulerHost Namespace QuartzRestApi Assembly QuartzRestApi.dll Hosts a self-contained REST API for managing and monitoring a Quartz.NET scheduler, providing endpoints for scheduling operations and status queries. Supports optional API key authentication and structured logging integration. public class SchedulerHost Inheritance object SchedulerHost Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Use SchedulerHost to expose a Quartz.NET IScheduler instance over HTTP via a Web API. The host can be configured with or without authentication, and supports both single and multiple API key profiles for access control. Logging can be integrated by providing an ILogger instance. The API is self-hosted using ASP.NET Core and Kestrel, and includes OpenAPI (Swagger) documentation for client discovery. Thread safety and lifecycle management are handled internally; callers should use Start and Stop to control the API's availability. Constructors SchedulerHost(string, IScheduler, ILogger) Creates a host without authentication (all endpoints publicly accessible). public SchedulerHost(string baseAddress, IScheduler scheduler, ILogger logger) Parameters baseAddress string The base address, e.g. http://localhost:45000. scheduler IScheduler Quartz.IScheduler logger ILogger Optional ILogger. SchedulerHost(string, IScheduler, ILogger, IEnumerable<ApiKeyProfile>) Creates a host with multiple named API key profiles. Each profile can optionally restrict which endpoints its bearer may call. public SchedulerHost(string baseAddress, IScheduler scheduler, ILogger logger, IEnumerable<ApiKeyProfile> profiles) Parameters baseAddress string The base address, e.g. http://localhost:45000. scheduler IScheduler Quartz.IScheduler logger ILogger Optional ILogger. profiles IEnumerable<ApiKeyProfile> One or more ApiKeyProfile instances. Pass null or an empty collection to disable authentication. SchedulerHost(string, IScheduler, ILogger, string) Creates a host with a single API key that grants full access to all endpoints. Backwards-compatible with the previous single-key API. public SchedulerHost(string baseAddress, IScheduler scheduler, ILogger logger, string apiKey) Parameters baseAddress string The base address, e.g. http://localhost:45000. scheduler IScheduler Quartz.IScheduler logger ILogger Optional ILogger. apiKey string A single API key with unrestricted access. It is thus considered a 'best practice' that the Execute code of a Job retrieve data from the JobDataMap found on this object. NOTE: Do not expect value 'set' into this JobDataMap to somehow be set back onto a job's own JobDataMap. Attempts to change the contents of this map typically result in an illegal state. index.html.title: QuartzRestApi | QuartzRestApi
index.html.summary:
QuartzRestApi A self-hosted REST API library for Quartz.NET, built on .NET 10 with ASP.NET Core / Kestrel. QuartzRestApi wraps your existing IScheduler instance behind an HTTP endpoint so other processes — on the same machine or across a network — can manage jobs and triggers without needing a direct Quartz.NET reference. Architecture graph TD subgraph App["Your Application"] HOST["SchedulerHost
Wraps ASP.NET Core
Kestrel web server"] CLIENT["SchedulerConnector
HttpClient-based C# client
for the REST API"] end subgraph API["REST API - Kestrel"] AUTH["ApiKeyMiddleware
Optional API key
+ profile-based access control"] CTRL["SchedulerController
/Scheduler/..."] end subgraph Quartz["Quartz.NET"] SCHED["IScheduler
- Jobs
- Triggers
- Calendars"] end HOST -- hosts --> AUTH AUTH -- authorized --> CTRL CLIENT -- "HTTP - GET / POST / DELETE
X-Api-Key header" --> AUTH CTRL -- delegates to --> SCHED Key components: Component Description SchedulerHost Starts an ASP.NET Core / Kestrel HTTP server that exposes the Quartz.NET scheduler as a REST API ApiKeyMiddleware Optional middleware that validates the X-Api-Key header and enforces per-profile route restrictions ApiKeyProfile A named profile that pairs an API key with an optional whitelist of allowed endpoints SchedulerController ASP.NET Core controller with all scheduler endpoints under the /Scheduler/ route prefix SchedulerConnector A typed C# HTTP client that wraps all REST calls so you do not need to craft HTTP requests manually Wrappers DTO classes (de)serialised to/from JSON for jobs, triggers, calendars, etc. Requirements .NET 10 or higher A configured Quartz.NET IScheduler How to host Quartz.NET via the REST API // No authentication -- all endpoints publicly accessible var host = new SchedulerHost("http://localhost:44344", scheduler, logger); host.Start(); scheduler -- your IScheduler instance from Quartz.NET logger -- any Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger (or null to disable logging) To stop the host: host.Stop(); How to connect to the host var connector = new SchedulerConnector("http://localhost:44344"); Use the SchedulerConnector methods to call the API from C# without dealing with raw HTTP. Security (API key authentication) Authentication is opt-in. When no key or profiles are configured every request passes through without any check. Single API key (full access) // Host var host = new SchedulerHost("http://localhost:44344", scheduler, logger, apiKey: "my-secret-key"); // Client var connector = new SchedulerConnector("http://localhost:44344", apiKey: "my-secret-key"); The key is sent and validated via the X-Api-Key HTTP header. Multiple API keys with profiles Each ApiKeyProfile pairs an API key with a set of strongly-typed boolean properties -- one per endpoint. Use ApiKeyProfile.AllowAll to start with full access and selectively disable endpoints, or use ApiKeyProfile.DenyAll to start with no access and selectively enable only what is needed. // Admin profile -- full access to all endpoints var admin = ApiKeyProfile.AllowAll("Admin", "key-admin-abc123"); // Read-only profile -- start with nothing allowed, then enable only query endpoints var readOnly = ApiKeyProfile.DenyAll("ReadOnly", "key-readonly-xyz"); readOnly.SchedulerName = true; readOnly.SchedulerInstanceId = true; readOnly.GetMetaData = true; readOnly.GetJobGroupNames = true; readOnly.GetTriggerGroupNames = true; readOnly.GetJobKeys = true; readOnly.GetJobDetail = true; readOnly.GetTrigger = true; readOnly.GetTriggerState = true; readOnly.GetCurrentlyExecutingJobs = true; // Monitoring profile -- start with full access, then remove mutating endpoints var monitoring = ApiKeyProfile.AllowAll("Monitoring", "key-mon-def456"); monitoring.Start = false; monitoring.StartDelayed = false; monitoring.Standby = false; monitoring.Shutdown = false; monitoring.Clear = false; monitoring.ScheduleJobWithJobDetailAndTrigger = false; monitoring.ScheduleJobIdentifiedWithTrigger = false; monitoring.ScheduleJobWithJobDetailAndTriggers = false; monitoring.ScheduleJobs = false; monitoring.RescheduleJob = false; monitoring.UnscheduleJob = false; monitoring.UnscheduleJobs = false; monitoring.AddJob = false; monitoring.DeleteJob = false; monitoring.DeleteJobs = false; monitoring.TriggerJobWithJobkey = false; monitoring.TriggerJobWithDataMap = false; monitoring.PauseJob = false; monitoring.PauseJobs = false; monitoring.PauseTrigger = false; monitoring.PauseTriggers = false; monitoring.PauseAllTriggers = false; monitoring.ResumeJob = false; monitoring.ResumeJobs = false; monitoring.ResumeTrigger = false; monitoring.ResumeTriggers = false; monitoring.ResumeAllTriggers = false; monitoring.AddCalendar = false; monitoring.DeleteCalendar = false; monitoring.ResetTriggerFromErrorState = false; var host = new SchedulerHost("http://localhost:44344", scheduler, logger, profiles: [admin, readOnly, monitoring]); Persisting profiles Profiles can be saved to and loaded from JSON: // Save File.WriteAllText("readOnly.json", readOnly.ToJson()); // Load var loaded = ApiKeyProfile.FromJson(File.ReadAllText("readOnly.json")); HTTP responses Situation Status No profiles configured Request passes through (no auth) X-Api-Key header missing 401 Unauthorized Key not recognised 401 Unauthorized Key valid, route allowed Request passes through Key valid, route not in whitelist 403 Forbidden Interactive API documentation (Scalar / OpenAPI) When the host is running, interactive API documentation is available at: URL Description /openapi/v1.json Raw OpenAPI 3 document /scalar/v1 Scalar interactive API reference Open http://localhost:44344/scalar/v1 in a browser to explore and test all endpoints without writing any code. The full hosted API reference is also available on this documentation site: REST API Reference Logging QuartzRestApi uses the Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger interface. Any compatible logging library works (Serilog, NLog, etc.). Log levels used: Information -- standard results (booleans, names, DateTimeOffsets) Debug -- full JSON request/response bodies Warning -- rejected requests (missing or invalid API key, forbidden route) License QuartzRestApi is Copyright (C) 2022 - 2026 Magic-Sessions and is licensed under the MIT license.
